title: Philippines President Marcos to make state visit to Japan
contents:
# Philippines President Marcos to make state visit to Japan

## Overview
Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. announced a state visit to Japan from May 26-29, marking the first such visit by a Philippine president in over a decade amid deepening military cooperation. Marcos will meet Emperor Naruhito, Empress Masako, and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to discuss the Philippines-Japan Strengthened Strategic Partnership, focusing on maritime security, energy, and food security. The announcement highlights recent military ties, including Japan's supply of coast guard ships and radar systems to the Philippines.

## Reporting Window
- Reported timeframe: 2026-04-24
- Location: Philippines

## Key Details
- Marcos and his wife Louise will remain received by Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako during the May 26-29 trip.
- Discussions with Prime Minister Takaichi will cover the future of the bilateral strategic partnership and international issues like energy, food, and maritime security.
- The visit builds on strengthening military ties, with Japan providing Manila coast guard ships and radar systems in recent years.
- A related development on April 28 saw Takaichi entrust a letter to Marcos via former PM Fumio Kishida, who visited Manila April 30-May 2 to advance energy cooperation and AZEC decarbonization framework.
- Kishida, as special envoy, aimed to boost economic energy resilience amid Middle East tensions.

## Source Context
The @BeltelFreeAudio account on X (Twitter) covers Belteleradio Free Audio's subject area as documented in this repository's source manifest. The development above appears in news reporting that aligns with the topical scope this account monitors. Verification stems from the cited publication, not from a direct tweet capture (Twitter API access remained blocked during this collection cycle).
